source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Figure is a model of a benzene molecule. All atoms lie in a plane, and the carbon atoms (\( m_{\text{C}} = 1.99 \times 10^{-26} \text{ kg} \)) form a regular hexagon, as do the hydrogen atoms (\( m_{\text{H}} = 1.67 \times 10^{-27} \text{ kg} \)). Calculate the moment of inertia of the molecule about an axis perpendicular to the plane of the paper through the center point \( O \).

image_dependency: Question explicitly references a figure and asks for a geometric/rotational quantity about center O
The text gives atom masses and hexagonal arrangement, but no explicit atom-center distances or bond lengths needed for the moment-of-inertia calculation
multi_step: Solving requires combining visual geometry from the figure with physics formula(s) for moment of inertia
The molecule has multiple mass groups (carbon atoms and hydrogen atoms) whose contributions must be summed
The task likely involves at least: extracting radii/geometry from the figure, then computing and aggregating rotational contributions
verifiability: answer_type=short_text with a single numeric target and units
Multiple-choice options are provided, giving an additional consistency check against the final value
registry_passed=true, answer_present=true, and the required image asset exists

decision_reasons: Core assets are complete: question text, answer, and the required primary image are all present.
registry_passed=true with no recorded asset issues, so there is no evident structural problem blocking recovery or use.
The sample is highly image-dependent, and the image asset exists, which preserves the core semantics needed for solving.
Verifiability is strong because an answer is present and the record indicates a single numeric target with units.
Risk flags are limited to dimensions not being stated in text and possible numeric rounding variation; these are manageable and do not justify deprioritizing a structurally complete sample.
